title: Task Manager
summary: Task Manager is a Windows utility used to view and manage running apps, background processes, performance, startup apps, users, details, and services.
facts: The Processes tab shows running apps and background processes.
The Performance tab shows live CPU, memory, disk, network, and GPU activity.
The Startup tab can enable or disable startup apps.
The Services tab can view services and open the full Services console.
examples: Use Task Manager to end a frozen app.
Use the Startup tab to reduce boot-time startup programs.
commonMistakes: Confusing Task Manager with Event Viewer. Task Manager shows current activity; Event Viewer shows logs.
Assuming disabling startup apps uninstalls them. It only prevents automatic startup.
examTips: For a frozen application, Task Manager is usually the first tool to end the task.
scenarios: A user says an application is frozen and will not close. Use Task Manager to end the process.
pbqIdeas: Identify which Task Manager tab should be used for startup apps, performance graphs, processes, or services.
sourceVideos: Professor Messer A+ Core 2 220-1202 - 07 Task Manager
